seoTitle: Mersen Fuse Products | High-Speed, DC, Industrial Fuse Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Mersen fuse products including high-speed fuses for semiconductor protection, DC fuses for EV and solar, and industrial fuses. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between high-speed fuses and standard fuses?
faqs.answer: High-speed fuses are specifically designed to protect semiconductors with ultra-fast opening times (<1ms) and low I2t values. Standard fuses are slower (10-100ms) and designed for general circuit protection. High-speed fuses actively limit peak fault current and control arc voltage to protect expensive semiconductor devices like IGBTs and diodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-speed fuses for semiconductor protection. Use standard fuses for general circuit protection.

faqs.question: How do I select the right fuse voltage rating?
faqs.answer: Select fuse voltage rating at least 20% above maximum system voltage including transients. For 400V systems, use 500V fuses. For 800V EV systems, use 1000V fuses. Always verify the fuse AC or DC rating matches your application - DC ratings are typically lower than AC for the same fuse.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage rating 20% above maximum system voltage. Verify AC/DC rating matches application.

faqs.question: What is I2t and why is it important for semiconductor protection?
faqs.answer: I2t (I-squared-t) is the measure of thermal energy that passes through a fuse during a fault. For semiconductor protection, the fuse I2t must be less than the semiconductor's withstand I2t. High-speed fuses have very low I2t values, limiting thermal stress on protected devices. Always verify I2t coordination in your protection design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure fuse I2t is less than semiconductor withstand I2t. Contact our FAE for coordination analysis.

faqs.question: What breaking capacity do I need for my application?
faqs.answer: Breaking capacity must exceed maximum prospective fault current. Typical requirements: 100kA for industrial power systems, 150kA-200kA for large semiconductor applications, 50kA-100kA for EV and energy storage. Calculate fault current based on power source characteristics and select fuse with at least 25% margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate maximum fault current and select fuse with 25% margin. Contact our FAE for assistance.

faqs.question: How do I coordinate fuses with other protection devices?
faqs.answer: Fuse coordination ensures selective protection - only the device closest to fault operates. Key principles: 1) Time-current curves should not overlap; 2) Downstream fuse operates faster than upstream; 3) I2t let-through of upstream exceeds melting I2t of downstream; 4) Maintain 2:1 current ratio between levels. Perform coordination study for complex systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Perform coordination study using time-current curves. Contact our FAE for complex systems.

categories.name: High-Speed Fuses
categories.description: Mersen high-speed fuses provide ultra-fast protection for power semiconductors including IGBTs, diodes, and thyristors. These fuses feature low I2t values, current limitation, and high breaking capacity up to 200kA. Designed for power converters, motor drives, UPS systems, and renewable energy inverters.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select High-Speed Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting high-speed fuses for semiconductor protection based on voltage, current, I2t coordination, and breaking capacity.

faqs.question: What makes high-speed fuses different from standard fuses?
faqs.answer: High-speed fuses open within milliseconds (<1ms) compared to 10-100ms for standard fuses. They have much lower I2t values, actively limit peak fault current, and control arc voltage to protect semiconductor junctions. Breaking capacity is typically higher (up to 200kA) for power electronics applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-speed fuses for all semiconductor protection applications.

faqs.question: How do I match fuse I2t to semiconductor withstand?
faqs.answer: The fuse total clearing I2t must be less than the semiconductor's withstand I2t at the same fault current. Check semiconductor datasheet for I2t rating (typically at 10ms). Select fuse with clearing I2t at least 20% below semiconductor rating. Consider temperature effects - both ratings may vary with temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare fuse clearing I2t to semiconductor withstand I2t with 20% margin.

products.name: 200A 500V High-Speed Fuse
products.shortDescription: 200A 500V high-speed fuse for IGBT protection with 150kA breaking capacity and ultra-low I2t.
descriptionParagraphs: The HS200-500 is a high-performance fuse designed for semiconductor protection in power electronics applications.
With ultra-fast acting characteristics and low I2t values, this fuse provides optimal protection for IGBTs and diodes.
The 150kA breaking capacity ensures reliable interruption of severe fault currents in power converter applications.
features: Low I2t minimizes thermal stress on IGBTs
Current limitation reduces peak fault current

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my 15 years supporting power electronics designs, I've found Mersen high-speed fuses to be among the most reliable for semiconductor protection. The HS series consistently delivers the low I2t values needed for modern IGBT modules. I particularly appreciate the consistent performance across temperature ranges - critical for industrial applications. For sizing, I always recommend comparing fuse clearing I2t to the IGBT's withstand rating with at least 20% margin. The HS200-500 is ideal for 100-150kW power converters using 600V IGBT modules.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable semiconductor protection with ultra-low I2t for IGBT applications

alternativeParts.comparison: HS200-500=><HS250-500: Output current 250A > 200A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating for increased power requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Larger power converters or increased safety margin

alternativeParts.comparison: HS200-500=><HS200-660: Output current 200A = 200A (same), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for Size 2 high-speed fuses

faqs.question: What is the typical opening time of this high-speed fuse?
faqs.answer: The HS200-500 typically opens in less than 1 millisecond at 10x rated current (2000A). At lower overcurrents (2-3x rating), opening time may be 5-20ms. The ultra-fast opening limits let-through I2t to protect semiconductors. Actual opening time depends on fault current magnitude - higher currents result in faster opening.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify opening time meets your semiconductor protection requirements. Contact our FAE for time-current curves.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the I2t for coordination with my IGBT?
faqs.answer: IGBT withstand I2t is typically specified in the datasheet at 10ms pulse width. For coordination: 1) Find IGBT I2t withstand from datasheet; 2) Find fuse clearing I2t at same fault current from time-current curve; 3) Ensure fuse I2t < 0.8 x IGBT I2t (20% margin); 4) Consider worst-case temperature. Example: If IGBT withstand is 50,000 A²s, fuse clearing should be <40,000 A²s.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare fuse clearing I2t to IGBT withstand with 20% margin. Use manufacturer curves for accurate values.

products.name: 100A 1000V High-Speed Fuse
products.shortDescription: 100A 1000V high-speed fuse for high-voltage semiconductor protection with 200kA breaking capacity.
descriptionParagraphs: The PC100-1000 is designed for high-voltage semiconductor protection in advanced power electronics.
With 1000V rating and ultra-low I2t, this fuse protects high-voltage IGBTs and SiC devices.
The 200kA breaking capacity handles severe faults in high-power applications.
features: Designed for SiC and high-voltage IGBT modules

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High Voltage Systems
faeReview.content: The PC series represents Mersen's advanced high-voltage fuse technology. I've specified the PC100-1000 for several 800V EV charging and energy storage projects. The 1000V rating with 200kA breaking capacity is exceptional for high-voltage semiconductor protection. For SiC applications, the ultra-low I2t is critical - SiC devices are fast but sensitive to overcurrent. I always verify the fuse voltage rating exceeds the maximum DC link voltage by at least 20%. The PC series is my go-to recommendation for any application above 600V DC.
faeReview.highlight: Ultimate high-voltage protection with 200kA breaking capacity for advanced power electronics

alternativeParts.comparison: PC100-1000=><PC150-1000: Output current 150A > 100A (+50%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for increased power requirements

companionParts.description: 80A version for lower current HV applications
Matching fuse holder for Size 3 PC fuses

faqs.question: Is this fuse suitable for SiC MOSFET protection?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PC100-1000 is excellent for SiC MOSFET protection. SiC devices have fast switching but lower overcurrent tolerance than silicon IGBTs. The PC series ultra-low I2t and fast opening time (<1ms) provide the protection needed for SiC devices. Verify the fuse I2t is less than the SiC MOSFET's avalanche energy rating. The 1000V rating accommodates 800V DC link systems common in SiC applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify fuse I2t < SiC device avalanche energy. Contact our FAE for SiC protection guidelines.

products.name: 150A 500V High-Speed Fuse
products.shortDescription: 150A 500V high-speed fuse for IGBT protection with 150kA breaking capacity and ultra-low I2t for power electronics.
descriptionParagraphs: The HS150-500 is a high-performance high-speed fuse designed for semiconductor protection in medium-power applications.
With 150A current rating and ultra-fast acting characteristics, this fuse provides reliable protection for IGBTs and power modules.
The 150kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe fault currents in industrial power converters.
features: Low I2t minimizes thermal stress on IGBTs
Wide temperature range for industrial use

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The HS150-500 is an excellent choice for medium-power applications using 75-100kW converters. The 150A rating provides good protection for common IGBT modules while maintaining adequate safety margin. I often recommend this fuse for solar inverters and motor drives where the continuous current is in the 100-120A range. The Size 2 footprint is compact and fits well in most power electronic enclosures. Always verify the fuse voltage rating exceeds your DC link voltage by at least 20%.
faeReview.highlight: Ideal for medium-power converters with 150A rating and compact Size 2 footprint

alternativeParts.comparison: HS150-500=><HS200-500: Output current 200A > 150A (+33%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating for increased power requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Larger power converters requiring higher current

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for Size 2 fuses

faqs.question: What is the typical opening time of the HS150-500?
faqs.answer: The HS150-500 typically opens in less than 1 millisecond at 10x rated current (1500A). At lower overcurrents (2-3x rating), opening time may be 5-15ms. The ultra-fast opening limits let-through I2t to protect semiconductors effectively.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify opening time meets your semiconductor protection requirements.

products.name: 300A 500V High-Speed Fuse
products.shortDescription: 300A 500V high-speed fuse for high-power IGBT protection with 150kA breaking capacity and ultra-low I2t.
descriptionParagraphs: The HS300-500 is a high-current high-speed fuse designed for high-power semiconductor protection in large power converters.
With 300A current rating and ultra-fast acting characteristics, this fuse provides robust protection for high-power IGBT modules.
The 150kA breaking capacity ensures reliable interruption of severe fault currents in high-power industrial applications.
features: High-current 300A rating for large power converters
Wide temperature range for industrial use

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High Power Systems
faeReview.content: The HS300-500 is my recommendation for high-power applications above 150kW. The 300A rating handles large IGBT modules commonly used in industrial drives and traction applications. The Size 3 body provides adequate thermal management for continuous 300A operation. I always ensure customers verify their cooling system can handle the fuse power dissipation at full current. For applications above 300A, consider parallel fuses or the larger PC series.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 300A rating ideal for large industrial drives and traction systems

alternativeParts.comparison: HS300-500=><HS200-500: Output current 200A < 300A (-33%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for smaller power requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Smaller power converters with lower current needs

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for Size 3 fuses

faqs.question: What is the power dissipation of the HS300-500 at rated current?
faqs.answer: The HS300-500 has typical power dissipation of 25-30W at rated 300A current. This must be considered in thermal design - ensure adequate heat sinking and ventilation in the fuse compartment. The fuse holder should be mounted on a thermally conductive surface.
faqs.decisionGuide: Account for 25-30W power dissipation in thermal design. Ensure adequate cooling.

categories.name: DC Fuses
categories.description: Mersen DC fuses provide reliable protection for high-voltage DC applications including electric vehicles, battery energy storage systems, and solar power installations. These fuses are specifically designed for DC interruption with ratings up to 1500VDC and breaking capacities up to 100kA. DC fuses feature specialized arc quenching technology for safe interruption of DC fault currents.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select DC Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting DC fuses for EV, energy storage, and solar applications based on voltage, current, and breaking capacity requirements.

faqs.question: Why do DC applications require special fuses?
faqs.answer: DC fuses require special design because DC arcs are harder to interrupt than AC arcs. DC fuses feature: 1) Enhanced arc quenching chambers; 2) Longer arc paths; 3) Specialized filler materials; 4) Higher breaking capacity ratings for DC; 5) Different time-current characteristics. AC fuses cannot safely interrupt DC faults and may fail catastrophically.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use DC-rated fuses for DC applications. Never use AC-only fuses on DC circuits.

products.name: 400A 1000V DC Fuse
products.shortDescription: 400A 1000VDC fuse for EV and energy storage with 100kA breaking capacity. Ideal for industrial and consumer applications with excellent performance and reliability.
descriptionParagraphs: The FD400-1000 is a high-performance DC fuse designed for electric vehicle and energy storage applications.
With 1000VDC rating and 400A continuous current, this fuse provides reliable protection for high-voltage battery systems.
The 100kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe DC fault currents.
features: Wide temperature range for automotive use

faeReview.author: Steven Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - DC Systems
faeReview.content: The FD series is my go-to recommendation for DC applications. The specialized DC arc quenching is critical - I've seen AC fuses fail spectacularly on DC circuits. For EV applications, the 1000V rating handles 800V nominal systems with margin. Breaking capacity is crucial for battery protection - large battery packs can deliver 50-100kA fault currents. I always verify the fuse DC rating matches the application - some fuses have different AC and DC ratings. For installation, proper torque is essential - 25-30 Nm for M10 terminals.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable DC protection with specialized arc quenching for EV and energy storage

alternativeParts.comparison: FD400-1000=><FD500-1000: Output current 500A > 400A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger battery systems
alternativeParts.useCase: Large EV or commercial energy storage

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for FD series

faqs.question: Can I use this fuse for AC applications?
faqs.answer: While DC fuses can typically be used for AC, the reverse is not true. The FD400-1000 is rated for 1000V DC but may have a higher AC rating (typically 690V AC). Check the datasheet for specific AC ratings. For mixed AC/DC applications, verify both ratings are adequate.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check datasheet for AC rating if needed. DC fuses generally work for AC but AC fuses cannot be used for DC.

products.name: 300A 1000V DC Fuse
products.shortDescription: 300A 1000VDC fuse for EV and energy storage with 100kA breaking capacity. Compact design for space-constrained applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The FD300-1000 is a compact DC fuse designed for electric vehicle and energy storage applications where space is limited.
With 1000VDC rating and 300A continuous current, this fuse provides reliable protection for medium-voltage battery systems.
The 100kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe DC fault currents in compact installations.
features: Wide temperature range for automotive use

faeReview.author: Steven Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - DC Systems
faeReview.content: The FD300-1000 is ideal for smaller EV and energy storage systems where 400A is not needed. The 300A rating is perfect for compact battery packs and auxiliary systems. I often recommend this fuse for commercial EV applications where space is at a premium. The same reliable DC arc quenching as the larger FD400 ensures safe operation.
faeReview.highlight: Compact 300A rating ideal for smaller EV and energy storage systems

alternativeParts.comparison: FD300-1000=><FD400-1000: Output current 400A > 300A (+33%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating for larger battery systems
alternativeParts.useCase: Large EV or commercial energy storage

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for FD series

faqs.question: What is the difference between FD300 and FD400?
faqs.answer: The main difference is current rating - FD300 is rated for 300A continuous current while FD400 is rated for 400A. Both have the same 1000VDC voltage rating and 100kA breaking capacity. The FD300 is more compact and suitable for smaller systems where 300A is sufficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FD300 for systems up to 250A continuous. Choose FD400 for systems up to 350A continuous.

products.name: 500A 1000V DC Fuse
products.shortDescription: 500A 1000VDC fuse for high-power EV and energy storage with 100kA breaking capacity for large battery systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The FD500-1000 is a high-current DC fuse designed for large electric vehicle and energy storage applications.
With 1000VDC rating and 500A continuous current, this fuse provides robust protection for high-power battery systems.
The 100kA breaking capacity ensures safe interruption of severe DC fault currents in large installations.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High Power DC
faeReview.content: The FD500-1000 is designed for the largest EV and energy storage applications. The 500A rating handles the highest power battery systems used in commercial vehicles and utility storage. The Size 4 body provides excellent thermal management for continuous 500A operation. Always ensure adequate cooling when using this fuse at full rating.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 500A rating for the largest EV and energy storage systems

alternativeParts.comparison: FD500-1000=><FD400-1000: Output current 400A < 500A (-20%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for smaller systems

companionParts.description: Matching fuse holder for Size 4 fuses

faqs.question: What applications require the FD500-1000?
faqs.answer: The FD500-1000 is designed for the highest power applications including commercial EVs, utility-scale energy storage, and high-power DC fast charging stations. It's typically used in systems with battery capacities above 100kWh or continuous currents above 350A.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use FD500 for systems above 350A continuous current. Use FD400 for systems below 350A.

categories.name: Industrial Fuses
categories.description: Mersen industrial fuses provide reliable overcurrent protection for general industrial applications including motor protection, power distribution, and control circuits. These fuses comply with IEC and UL standards for industrial electrical installations with ratings up to 690V and breaking capacities up to 100kA.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Industrial Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting industrial fuses for motor protection, power distribution, and general industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between gG and aM fuse types?
faqs.answer: gG (general purpose) fuses provide full-range protection against overloads and short-circuits. aM (motor) fuses provide short-circuit protection only and must be used with overload relays. gG fuses operate at lower overcurrents (1.6x rating), while aM fuses allow motor starting currents (4-8x rating). Use gG for general circuits, aM for motor circuits with separate overload protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gG for general circuits. Use aM for motor circuits with overload relays.

products.name: 250A 690V Industrial Fuse
products.shortDescription: 250A 690V gG industrial fuse for motor protection and power distribution with 100kA breaking capacity.
descriptionParagraphs: The NH250-690 is a standard industrial fuse for motor protection and power distribution applications.
With 690V rating and gG characteristic, this fuse provides full-range protection for industrial circuits.
The 100kA breaking capacity handles severe fault currents in industrial power systems.
features: Standard NH size for easy installation
Visual indicator for blown fuse detection

faeReview.author: Robert Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Systems
faeReview.content: The NH series is the workhorse of industrial fuse protection. I've specified NH fuses for countless industrial projects over 20 years. The standard sizes make replacement easy - any manufacturer's NH2 fuse fits in any NH2 holder. For motor protection, remember that gG fuses provide overload protection while aM fuses only protect against short-circuits. I typically recommend gG for general circuits and aM for large motors with separate overload relays. The visual indicator is helpful for maintenance - you can see if a fuse has blown without removing it.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable industrial protection with standard NH mounting for easy maintenance

alternativeParts.comparison: NH250-690=><NH315-690: Output current 315A > 250A (+26%), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: NH2 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: Can NH fuses from different manufacturers be interchanged?
faqs.answer: Yes, NH fuses are standardized per DIN 43620 and IEC 60269-1. Any manufacturer's NH2 fuse will fit in any NH2 holder. However, electrical characteristics may vary slightly between manufacturers. For critical applications, use fuses from the same manufacturer throughout the system for consistent performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: NH fuses are interchangeable. Use same manufacturer for consistent performance in critical systems.

products.name: 315A 690V Industrial Fuse
products.shortDescription: 315A 690V gG industrial fuse for high-power motor protection and power distribution with 100kA breaking capacity.
descriptionParagraphs: The NH315-690 is a high-current industrial fuse for large motor protection and power distribution applications.
With 690V rating and 315A current rating, this fuse provides full-range protection for high-power industrial circuits.
The 100kA breaking capacity handles severe fault currents in large industrial power systems.
features: Standard NH2 size for easy installation
Visual indicator for blown fuse detection

faeReview.author: Robert Johnson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Systems
faeReview.content: The NH315-690 is commonly used for large motor protection in industrial applications. The 315A rating is ideal for motors in the 150-200kW range. I always recommend using gG fuses for motor circuits with separate overload relays. The NH2 size is standard across all manufacturers, making replacement easy.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 315A rating ideal for large industrial motor protection

alternativeParts.comparison: NH315-690=><NH250-690: Output current 250A < 315A (-20%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for smaller motors

companionParts.description: NH2 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: What motor size is the NH315-690 suitable for?
faqs.answer: The NH315-690 is suitable for three-phase motors in the 150-200kW range at 400V, or 200-250kW at 690V. Always verify the motor full-load current and starting current to ensure proper protection coordination.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for motors 150-200kW at 400V. Verify motor current ratings before selection.

features: Standard NH2 size for easy installation

faeReview.highlight: Versatile 200A rating for common industrial motor applications

alternativeParts.comparison: NH200-690=><NH250-690: Output current 250A > 200A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating for larger motors

faqs.question: What motor size is the NH200-690 suitable for?
faqs.answer: The NH200-690 is suitable for three-phase motors in the 90-110kW range at 400V. It's a very common rating for general industrial motor protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for motors 90-110kW at 400V. Most common industrial motor fuse rating.

categories.name: Surge Protection Devices
categories.description: Mersen surge protection devices (SPDs) protect electrical equipment from voltage surges caused by lightning strikes, switching transients, and power system disturbances. These devices feature high surge current capacity, fast response times, and reliable protection for critical equipment in industrial, commercial, and residential applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Surge Protection
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting surge protection devices based on voltage, surge current capacity, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What surge current rating do I need?
faqs.answer: Surge current rating depends on exposure level: 1) Service entrance - 100kA to 300kA per phase; 2) Distribution panels - 50kA to 100kA; 3) Equipment protection - 20kA to 50kA. Higher exposure areas (coastal, high lightning) need higher ratings. Use coordinated protection with higher ratings at service entrance and lower ratings downstream.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size based on exposure level and location in electrical system. Use coordinated protection.

products.name: 120kA 3-Phase Surge Protector
products.shortDescription: 120kA per phase surge protection device for industrial and commercial applications with 3-phase protection.
descriptionParagraphs: The ST120-3P provides comprehensive surge protection for 3-phase electrical systems.
With 120kA surge current capacity per phase, this SPD protects against severe lightning strikes and switching transients.
The compact DIN rail mounting allows easy installation in electrical panels.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Quality
faeReview.content: Surge protection is often overlooked but critical for equipment reliability. I've seen countless equipment failures from inadequate surge protection. The ST series provides excellent protection at a reasonable cost. For industrial applications, I always recommend at least 100kA rating at the service entrance. The visual indicator and remote contacts are valuable for maintenance - you can monitor protection status without panel access. Remember that SPDs degrade over time from repeated surges - plan for replacement every 5-10 years depending on exposure.
faeReview.highlight: High-capacity surge protection with fast response for critical equipment

alternativeParts.reason: Higher surge capacity for severe exposure

faqs.question: How often should surge protectors be replaced?
faqs.answer: SPDs should be replaced when: 1) Visual indicator shows failure; 2) After major surge events; 3) Every 5-10 years depending on exposure. MOVs (metal oxide varistors) degrade over time from repeated surges even without complete failure. High exposure areas may need more frequent replacement. Monitor status indicators regularly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Replace when indicator shows failure or every 5-10 years. Monitor status regularly.

products.name: 200kA 3-Phase Surge Protector
products.shortDescription: 200kA per phase surge protection device for high-exposure industrial applications with maximum protection.
descriptionParagraphs: The ST200-3P provides maximum surge protection for critical 3-phase electrical systems in high-exposure areas.
With 200kA surge current capacity per phase, this SPD protects against severe lightning strikes and major switching transients.
The rugged design is ideal for industrial environments with high lightning exposure.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Quality
faeReview.content: The ST200-3P is our highest capacity surge protector for the most demanding applications. I recommend this for service entrance protection in high lightning areas and critical facilities. The 200kA rating provides maximum protection against direct lightning strikes. It's more expensive than lower capacity units, but the protection is unmatched.
faeReview.highlight: Maximum 200kA surge protection for critical and high-exposure applications

faqs.question: When should I use the ST200 instead of ST120?
faqs.answer: Use ST200 for: 1) Service entrance protection; 2) High lightning exposure areas (coastal, mountain); 3) Critical facilities where downtime is unacceptable; 4) Areas with frequent thunderstorms. The extra capacity provides better protection against severe events.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ST200 for service entrance and high-exposure. Use ST120 for standard applications.

products.name: 80kA 3-Phase Surge Protector
products.shortDescription: 80kA per phase surge protection device for downstream protection in industrial and commercial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ST80-3P provides cost-effective surge protection for 3-phase electrical systems at distribution panels.
With 80kA surge current capacity per phase, this SPD protects against typical lightning strikes and switching transients.
The compact design is ideal for distribution panels and branch circuit protection.
features: 80kA surge current capacity for distribution panels

faeReview.author: Jennifer Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Quality
faeReview.content: The ST80-3P is perfect for downstream protection in a coordinated surge protection scheme. Use it at distribution panels after a higher capacity SPD at the service entrance. The 80kA rating is sufficient for most distribution applications and the cost is lower than high-capacity units.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 80kA protection for distribution panels and downstream locations

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacity for more severe exposure
alternativeParts.useCase: Higher exposure areas or upstream locations

faqs.question: Where should the ST80 be installed in a surge protection scheme?
faqs.answer: The ST80 is designed for downstream protection in a coordinated scheme. Install it at: 1) Distribution panels; 2) Branch circuits; 3) After a higher capacity SPD (ST120 or ST200) at service entrance. This provides layered protection throughout the electrical system.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ST80 at distribution panels with ST120/ST200 at service entrance for coordinated protection.
